annotations.label: Empty set

anchor.snippet: the empty set or void set is the unique set having no elements

annotations.note: The empty set is the EmptyCollection instance on Set α, satisfying x ∉ ∅ (Set.mem_empty_iff_false).

annotations.label: Non-empty set

anchor.snippet: Any set other than the empty set is called non-empty

annotations.note: Set.Nonempty s is defined as ∃ x, x ∈ s in Mathlib/Data/Set/Defs.lean.

annotations.label: Null set (measure theory)

anchor.snippet: null set is a distinct notion within the context of measure theory

annotations.note: Mathlib expresses 'null set' via μ s = 0 and NullMeasurableSet, but has no separate type or definition named NullSet.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of the empty set

anchor.snippet: there can be only one set with no elements

annotations.note: Set extensionality plus this lemma show any set with no elements equals ∅.

annotations.label: Subsets and power set of the empty set

anchor.snippet: The only subset of the empty set is the empty set itself

annotations.note: Set.powerset_empty states 𝒫 (∅ : Set α) = {∅}, and Set.subset_empty_iff gives s ⊆ ∅ ↔ s = ∅.

annotations.label: Operations with the empty set

anchor.snippet: The empty set is a subset of A

annotations.note: Mathlib has Set.union_empty, Set.empty_union, Set.inter_empty, Set.empty_inter covering the standard identities.

annotations.label: Vacuous truth for the empty set

anchor.snippet: the property P holds ( vacuous truth )

annotations.note: Set.forall_mem_empty states (∀ x ∈ (∅ : Set α), p x) ↔ True.

annotations.label: Empty set is a subset of any set

anchor.snippet: the empty set is a subset of any set A

annotations.note: Set.empty_subset : (∅ : Set α) ⊆ s is the direct formalization.

annotations.label: Zero as the empty set

anchor.snippet: zero is modelled by the empty set

annotations.note: ZFSet.empty exists, but Mathlib's Nat/Ordinal are not defined via the Von Neumann encoding 0 = ∅.

annotations.label: Empty sum and empty product

anchor.snippet: the sum of the elements of the empty set (the empty sum ) is zero

annotations.note: Finset.sum_empty and Finset.prod_empty give ∑/∏ over ∅ as 0/1 respectively.

annotations.label: Empty set as a derangement

anchor.snippet: The empty set can be considered a derangement of itself

annotations.note: derangements α is defined, but no specific lemma asserts the empty permutation is a derangement of the empty type.

annotations.label: Bounds of the empty set in ordered sets

anchor.snippet: every member of that set will be an upper bound and lower bound for the empty set

annotations.note: upperBounds_empty : upperBounds (∅ : Set α) = univ (and the dual) capture this.

annotations.label: Sup and inf of the empty set in extended reals

anchor.snippet: the least upper bound (sup or supremum ) of the empty set is negative infinity

annotations.note: sSup_empty = ⊥ in any complete lattice; instantiated at EReal (a CompleteLinearOrder) gives sSup ∅ = -∞.

annotations.label: Empty set is clopen and compact

anchor.snippet: the empty set is open by definition

annotations.note: isOpen_empty, isClosed_empty (Mathlib.Topology.Basic), and isCompact_empty (Mathlib.Topology.Compactness.Compact) cover all three.

annotations.label: Indiscrete topology

anchor.snippet: said to have the indiscrete topology if the only open sets

annotations.note: IndiscreteTopology α is the typeclass asserting the topology on α equals ⊤.

annotations.label: Closure of the empty set

anchor.snippet: The closure of the empty set is empty

annotations.note: closure_empty : closure (∅ : Set X) = ∅.

annotations.label: Empty function and initial object in Set

anchor.snippet: the empty set is the unique initial object of the category of sets and functions

annotations.note: Types.isInitialPEmpty exhibits PEmpty as an initial object of the category of types.

annotations.label: Empty space as strict initial object in Top

anchor.snippet: This empty topological space is the unique initial object in the category of topological spaces

annotations.note: TopCat.isInitialPEmpty witnesses the empty topological space as initial in TopCat (used in IsInducing.empty).

annotations.label: Von Neumann construction of ordinals

anchor.snippet: 0 is defined as the empty set, and the successor of an ordinal is defined as

annotations.note: Mathlib's Ordinal is built from equivalence classes of well-orders, not via the Von Neumann hierarchy of ZF sets.

annotations.label: Cantor's disjoint sets

anchor.snippet: Cantor defined two sets as being disjoint if their intersection has an absence of points

annotations.note: Mathlib defines Disjoint generally (Mathlib.Order.Disjoint) and Set.disjoint_iff_inter_eq_empty matches Cantor's intersection-empty characterization.

annotations.label: Existence and uniqueness of empty set in Zermelo set theory

anchor.snippet: the existence of the empty set is assured by the axiom of empty set

annotations.note: ZFSet.empty constructs the empty ZFC-set (with notMem_empty/eq_empty), but Mathlib does not axiomatize Zermelo's system or its axiom of empty set explicitly.
